diagnosis.differential_diagnosis: Greig Cephalopolysyndactyly (GCPS), Preaxial polydactyly type IV, polysyndactyly type A (PAP-A), oral-facio-digital syndrome (OFDS)
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis was confirmed based on clinical findings including hypothalamic hamartoma, postaxial polydactyly, syndactyly, and other anomalies. Molecular analysis in Patient 2 revealed a GLI3 mutation (c.3386_3387delTT), which is associated with PHS. The presence of skeletal dysplasia features such as fibular hypoplasia, radio-ulnar bowing, and proximal epiphyseal hypoplasia further supported the diagnosis.
